headline: Penn State travels to Athens seeking statement win
subhead: Two 3-3 teams fighting for bowl positioning collide in a rankings mismatch.

xFactor.why: Penn State's defense held Colorado to 14 points last week and must replicate that intensity to slow Georgia's offensive tempo.

keyMatchup: Penn State's rush defense versus Georgia's ground game will determine whether the Nittany Lions can control clock and limit big plays.
body: Penn State arrives in Athens riding confidence from a 24-point dismantling of Colorado, a performance that showed the team can execute when it matters. That defensive dominance will be the visitors' only real advantage against a Georgia program that moves differently at home. Georgia's win over Clemson revealed an offense capable of sustaining drives and converting red zone opportunities, two areas where Penn State's defense has been vulnerable outside last week's outlier performance.

Both teams sit at 3-3 and need this win for postseason credibility, but Georgia's ranking reflects what the coaching room has built here over time. The Bulldogs' home field advantage and recent offensive rhythm give them the edge, though Penn State's defensive line could create problems up front if it maintains last week's intensity. This shapes as a low-scoring, field-position battle that likely turns on a second-half adjustment from whichever staff executes more cleanly in the moment.
